title: Build librarian agent + real Library-layer viability measurement
description: Operator directive (2026-07-14): stop designing, start implementing/exercising the MAP 6.13 corpus -- specifically called out missing wikilinks in MAP docs and no librarian agent. Builds the first real librarian agent (scripts/librarian.py): wikilink resolution/validation with ambiguous-stem disambiguation, backlink index, and a real (not simulated) compression/churn measurement runner satisfying map-library-viability-measurement.md's previously-never-run prerequisite. Applies wikilinks to the 16 root *_SYSTEM.md/permission/policy docs as a bounded, additive, reversible proof (backtick paths preserved, wikilinks added alongside). Read map-library-viability-measurement.md, SUMMARY-0002-map-library-tool-candidates.md, and the 16 target docs' existing Related-Files sections before editing.
acceptance_criteria: librarian.py resolves [[wikilinks]] by stem, disambiguates ambiguous stems (e.g. multiple README.md files) via full relative path, and validates a repo scan for broken/ambiguous links.
wikilink conversion is additive (never removes existing backtick paths) and idempotent (re-running does not duplicate links).
Real compression-ratio and file-churn measurements are run against actual MAP docs (not simulated), with results and honest limitations recorded in a results artifact.
All 16 root system/policy docs have wikilinks added to their Related-Files sections with zero broken or ambiguous links per librarian.py validate.
